epic: Phase CFA-02 — KPI Cluster Export Widening
story: STORY CFA-02 — KPI cluster export widening
description: Wire the CFA-01 cluster_tree.export rescue primitive into the Performance (KPI) module so a cluster admin with Capability::KPIS_EXPORT + Capability::CLUSTER_TREE_EXPORT can export KPIs across descendant organizations. Mirrors the 9-D-D1a read widening pattern, but for the EXPORT surface. Read-only by design — no widening to update / create / delete / import.

userStories.title: KPI cluster export widening (KPI list export only)
acceptanceCriteria: Capability::KPIS_EXPORT = 'kpis.export' constant declared in Capability.php.

UserKpiScope::clusterVisibleOrgIds() widens when actor holds KPIS_EXPORT + CLUSTER_TREE_EXPORT (purpose='export') on actor.organization_id. Existing KPIS_VIEW + CLUSTER_TREE_VIEW widening (purpose=null/'view') preserved for reads.

KpiController::export() authz: 'export' ability accepts EITHER KPIS_EXPORT OR KPIS_VIEW (backward compat for same-org export). Cross-org export requires KPIS_EXPORT + CLUSTER_TREE_EXPORT (strict per CFA-00 contract via scope).
KpiController::filteredKpiQuery accepts purpose parameter; export endpoint calls with purpose='export' so the export-pair widening applies (not the view-pair widening).
Cluster admin (KPIS_EXPORT + CLUSTER_TREE_EXPORT) export endpoint includes KPIs from descendant organizations in the CSV/XLSX stream.
User without KPIS_EXPORT (only KPIS_VIEW) export endpoint stays strict same-org (no descendant KPIs in stream).
User without CLUSTER_TREE_EXPORT (only KPIS_EXPORT) export endpoint stays strict same-org.
User with KPIS_VIEW + CLUSTER_TREE_VIEW (read pair) export endpoint stays strict same-org per CFA-00 strict contract — read pair does NOT widen exports.
Sibling cluster user denied descendant KPIs in export stream.
Child user denied parent cluster KPIs in export stream (one-directional).
Null-org user fail-closed (empty export).
Super admin sees all KPIs in export regardless of cluster_tree grants.
All KPI write endpoints stay strict same-org (store / update / destroy / import / measurement / link).

No AccessDecision engine change (no modification of clusterTreeRescueApplies / crossOrgClusterTreeAdmitted / sameOrganization / extractOrganizationId / buildScopeChain / ScopeAssignmentResolver / any OrgGuard).
No migration. No CapabilityAlias entry needed (no legacy flat string for 'kpis.export').
No OVR / Surveys / HR / ActivityLog / Tasks / Projects / Risks / Meetings touches.
Direction R preserved (no legacy decisions routes restored, no manageMembers restored).
PR #6 / KPI 9-D-D1a read widening behavior not regressed (ClusterTreeKpiPolicyTest + ClusterTreeUserKpiScopeTest stay green).
CFA-01 engine primitive tests (ClusterTreeManageExportPrimitiveTest 19 tests) stay green.

All non-target regression tests stay green (UserKpiScopeTest, KpiPolicyTest, KpiMeasurementPolicyTest, KpiLinkPolicyTest, KpiOrgGuardTest, KpiControllerTest, KpiEngineAuthzTest, KpiRelationsTest, KpiValueIntegrityTest, ImprovementKpiRequiredTest).
userStories.referenceImplementation: CFA-01 (branch phase-cfa-01-cluster-tree-manage-export-primitives, PR #10, merge commit 7dc6e44) for the engine primitive. Phase 9-D-D1a (branch phase-9d-d1a-kpi-cluster-tree-read, PR #6, merge commit 79eb65e) for the read-widening pattern to mirror.

nonGoals: Widening KPI write endpoints (store / update / destroy / import / measurement / link create-delete) — all stay strict same-org.
Widening export in any other module (Projects / Risks / Meetings / Users / Strategy — each is its own CFA-N story).
Adding CLUSTER_TREE_EXPORT wiring to other modules' controllers (CFA-03+).
AccessDecision engine changes (engine is stable per CFA-01 contract).
Migrations (KPIS_EXPORT is a pure RBAC string constant).
Tasks / OVR / Surveys / HR / ActivityLog touches (excluded or deferred).
Changing the audit-trail label 'cluster_tree_rescue' (audit discrimination stays via capability string).
Introduce a KPIS_EXPORT legacy alias in CapabilityAlias (no legacy flat string exists).
stopConditions: PII exposure risk appears (KPIs do not carry sensitive PII, but verify no measurement payload includes patient-level fields)
Write path would be widened (e.g. KpiController::import, store, update, destroy)
Existing resource response includes sensitive fields (verify KpiImportExportService::rowFor() payload — should be the 14 documented columns only)
Tests require deleting or weakening real security coverage
Any conflict with Direction R / Direction B / 9-D-D1a KPI cluster_tree behavior / CFA-01 engine primitive contract
Any CI failure that cannot be explained by the current story
Existing admin user with KPIS_VIEW loses access to the existing export endpoint (regression)
KPIS_VIEW + CLUSTER_TREE_VIEW widens exports cross-org after CFA-02 (only KPIS_EXPORT + CLUSTER_TREE_EXPORT should widen exports)
mergeApproval: Autonomous via the CFA mode — auto-merge if all GitHub CI checks green and no stop condition triggered.
